titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the History of Medicine and Science Organizations and Institutions Collection
History of medicine and science organizations and institutions collection
titlestmt.author: Katherine Isham

publicationstmt.publisher: Medical Historical Library, Cushing/Whitney Medical Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Medical Historical Library, Cushing/Whitney Medical Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday May 22, 2026 at 22:00

did.unittitle: History of medicine and science organizations and institutions collection

corpname.part: Medical Historical Library, Cushing/Whitney Medical Library

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The collection is open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: The collection was created by the Medical Historical Library in 2025. Many of the materials were mailed to individual Medical Historical Library staff by organizations and institutions and include mailing labels identifying original recipients. Provenance for other items in the collection is unknown. Materials from the History of medicine and science newsletters collection, 1980-present, are included in this collection.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The collection is organized in five series: Series I. History of medicine and science organizations, United States, 1954-2009. Series II. History of medicine and science institutions, United States, 1840-2019. Series III. History of medicine and science organizations and institutions, England, 1878-2006. Series IV. History of medicine and science organizations and institutions, Canada, 1953-2013. Series V. History of medicine and science organizations and institutions, other countries, 1928-2019.

Materials in Series I. History of medicine and science organizations, United States, 1954-2009; III. History of medicine and science organizations and institutions, England, 1878-2006; and IV. History of medicine and science organizations and institutions, Canada, 1953-2013, are organized alphabetically by name. Materials in Series II. History of medicine and science institutions, United States, 1840-2019, are first organized by state, then alphabetically by name. Materials in Series V. History of medicine and science organizations and institutions, other countries, 1928-2019, are first organized by country, then alphabetically by name.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: History of medicine and science organizations and institutions collection. Historical Library, Harvey Cushing / John Hay Whitney Medical Library, Yale University.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection includes materials from history of medicine and science professional organizations, libraries, archives, museums, and foundations, 1909-2019.

Series I. History of medicine and science organizations, United States, 1954-2009, includes newsletters and conference materials from professional organizations based in the United States. Organizations include the American Association for the History of Medicine (AAHM), Archivists and Librarians in the History of the Health Sciences (ALHHS), the History of Science Society (HSS). and others. Some of the conference materials in the series are from events attended by Medical Historical Library staff.

Series II. History of medicine and science institutions, United States, 1840-2019, includes annual reports, exhibition publications, newsletters, bibliographies, aquisitions lists, guides, and events ephemera from numerous libraries, archives, museums, and foundations in the United States.

Series III: History of medicine and science institutions and organizations, England, 1878-2006, includes newsletters and conference materials from professional organizations and annual reports, exhibition publications, newsletters, bibliographies, aquisitions lists, guides, and events ephemera from libraries, archives, museums, and foundations in England. Institutions represented in the series include the Bodleian Library, Oxford; Royal College of Physicians, London; the Wellcome Institute, London, and others.

Series IV: History of medicine and science institutions and organizations, Canada, 1953-2013, includes newsletters and conference materials from professional organizations and annual reports, exhibition publications, newsletters, bibliographies, aquisitions lists, guides, and events ephemera from libraries, archives, museums, and foundations in Canada. The bulk of materials in the series are from the Osler Library, McGill University, Montreal.

Series V: History of medicine and science institutions and organizations, other countries, 1928-2019, includes newsletters and conference materials from professional organizations and annual reports, exhibition publications, newsletters, bibliographies, aquisitions lists, guides, and events ephemera from libraries, archives, museums, and foundations in Argentina, Australia, Austria, Belgium, Denmark, France, Germany, Hungary, Ireland, Japan, Mexico, The Netherlands, Poland, Scotland, South Africa, Spain, Sweden, and Switzerland.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright status for collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.
